content:
No manual code. Just prompts. The first 80% felt like magic the last 20% nearly broke me.

4 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I gave myself one rule: build a full Angular app without typing a single line of code myself.
Every component, every service, every fix AI only. I’d prompt, paste, run, repeat.
Two hours in, I had a working app and a big grin. Six hours in, I understood exactly what AI can and can’t do. Here’s the honest report.
The Problem
“AI can build apps now” is the loudest claim on the internet.
But there’s a gap between a flashy demo and a codebase you’d actually ship:

• Demos show the happy path. Real apps have edge cases.

• AI writes confident code. Confident isn’t the same as correct.

• “It runs” and “it’s maintainable” are completely different bars.
I wanted to find out where that gap actually is in Angular, specifically.
Why Developers Struggle With This
Most devs try AI-only builds and quit frustrated. Not because AI is bad because they prompt it wrong.
